evidence: Code block reads: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iOfficeAI/OfficeCLI/main/install.sh | bash` in the Install section; Claude Code agents following this skill will execute this verbatim
description: Skill instructs Claude to pipe a remote shell script directly to bash with no integrity check
suggested_fix: Download to /tmp, verify SHA256 against a published checksum, then install; or publish officecli to Homebrew/npm to eliminate the raw-GitHub bootstrap entirely

evidence: Code block reads: `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iOfficeAI/OfficeCLI/main/install.ps1 | iex` — PowerShell download-and-execute with no integrity check
description: Skill instructs Claude to pipe a remote PowerShell script directly to Invoke-Expression on Windows
suggested_fix: Use winget, choco, or a pinned/verified MSI download instead of irm|iex

description: Script header documents its own canonical install method as `curl -fsSL .../install-ubuntu.sh | bash`; the script is the target of that curl-pipe-sh invocation
suggested_fix: Document a package-manager or verified-download alternative alongside the curl-pipe-sh shortcut; add SHA256 verification step

description: Skill instructs: `curl -s https://skills.aionui.com/SKILL.md > ~/.config/aionui-skills/SKILL.md` — fetches remote content to a local config path with no integrity verification; a compromised CDN could deliver malicious skill instructions
suggested_fix: Add a SHA256 verification step after download, or serve the file over a content-addressed URL such as a pinned GitHub release asset

evidence: File opens with `You are an AI assistant` prose at line 1; no `---` delimiter present anywhere in the file
description: Agent file has no YAML frontmatter block; name field is absent and agent cannot be registered by Claude Code
suggested_fix:
Add `---
name: hello-researcher
description: Example research agent for the hello-world extension.
model: claude-sonnet-4-5
---` at line 1

evidence: No frontmatter block present; description field is absent
description: Agent file has no YAML frontmatter block; description field is absent
suggested_fix: See R01 fix — add complete frontmatter block including description

evidence: No `## Output` or equivalent output-format section found anywhere in the file
description: Agent specifies no output format; Claude response structure will be unpredictable
suggested_fix: Add an `## Output` section describing expected response shape (e.g. JSON, markdown, prose)

evidence: No `## Examples` section or equivalent found anywhere in the file
description: Agent has zero worked examples; expected invocation and output are undefined
suggested_fix: Add an `## Examples` section with at least one concrete invocation scenario

evidence: No model field present (frontmatter absent entirely)
description: Agent has no model pin; Claude Code will use default model selection

description: Multi-step command that reads files and runs shell commands has no allowed-tools declaration; tool-level sandboxing cannot be enforced
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Edit]` to frontmatter once frontmatter is added

description: Command uses $ARGUMENTS but has no guard for the empty-input case; behavior on bare invocation is undefined
suggested_fix: Add an explicit guard: if arguments are empty, print usage and exit with a clear error message

evidence: Line contains `/Users/veryliu/Documents/GitHub/officecli/` — macOS-specific absolute home-directory path belonging to the original author
description: Command references absolute developer-local path `/Users/veryliu/Documents/GitHub/officecli/`; will fail for all other contributors
suggested_fix: Replace with a relative path, an environment variable (e.g. `$OFFICECLI_REPO`), or auto-detect repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`

evidence: package-assistant.md explicitly states: 'Skills with # officecli: vX.X.X comments inside the YAML frontmatter block break AionUi\'s parser, causing skills to not appear in Skills Center'
description: YAML comment `# officecli: v1.0.24` inside the `---` block breaks AionUi's frontmatter parser; skill invisible in Skills Center
suggested_fix: Remove `# officecli: vX.X.X` from inside the `---` block; place version as HTML comment after closing `---` if tracking is needed

evidence: Both agent files under examples/hello-world-extension/agents/ lack frontmatter while all production agents in the repo have frontmatter
description: Both example agents in hello-world-extension were not updated when the frontmatter convention was adopted; contributors using these as templates will produce non-conforming agents
suggested_fix: Add minimal frontmatter to both example agent files and note the frontmatter convention in the example's README
